attributes: ENA first public: 2026-05-06; ENA-CHECKLIST: ERC000011; INSDC center name: McGill-D2R; INSDC status: public; Submitter Id: E-MTAB-15699:Sample 6; broker name: ArrayExpress; cell line: BIN-67; collection date: not collected; common name: human; disease: ovarian small cell carcinoma; genotype: SMARCA4; geographic location (country and/or sea): not collected; isolate: not applicable; organism part: ovary; sample name: E-MTAB-15699:Sample 6; scientific_name: Homo sapiens
metadata_text: Sample 6 Homo sapiens BioSample: SAMEA120315785; SRA: ERS26977772 <BioSample access="public" publication_date="2026-05-06T00:00:00.000" last_update="2026-05-06T01:00:13.000" submission_date="2026-05-07T08:11:25.920" id="59256381" accession="SAMEA120315785"> <Ids> <Id db="BioSample" is_primary="1">SAMEA120315785</Id> <Id db="SRA">ERS26977772</Id> </Ids> <Description> <Title>Sample 6</Title> <Organism taxonomy_id="9606" taxonomy_name="Homo sapiens"> <OrganismName>Homo sapiens</OrganismName> </Organism> <Comment> <Paragraph>Protocols: BIN-67, BIN-67 with inducible SMARCA4 restoration were cultured in Roswell Park Memorial Institute 1640 Medium (RPMI, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 11875-093; no pyruvate) with 6% tetracycline free fetal bovine serum (Wisent Inc., Cat# 081150), 1% penicillin/streptomycin (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 15140-122), and 2 mM L-glutamine (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 25030-081). Cells were maintained in a humidified 5% CO2-containing incubator at 37°C and regular Mycoplasma test was performed using Mycoalert Detection Kit (Lonza, Cat# LT07-318). Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Antibodies against H3K27ac (ab4729, Abcam), HDAC2 (ab124974, Abcam), p300 (ab14984, Abcam), HDAC1 (40967, Active Motif), p53(sc-126, Santa Cruz Biotechnology) were used for chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) experiments following a protocol with MNase. Briefly, the cell pellet was resuspended in 1 mL buffer A (25 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.5; 10 mmol/L KCl; 0.1% NP-40; and 1.5 mmol/L MgCl2) and homogenized. Nine volumes of buffer B (15 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.9; 15 mmol/L NaCl; 60 mmol/L KCl; 0.5 mmol/L phenylmethylsulfonylfluoride; and 0.32 mol/L sucrose) were added, and the mixture was centrifuged at 2,000 rpm (at 4°C for 7 minutes) to collect nuclei. title: Sample 17

attributes: ENA first public: 2026-05-06; ENA-CHECKLIST: ERC000011; INSDC center name: McGill-D2R; INSDC status: public; Submitter Id: E-MTAB-15700:Sample 17; broker name: ArrayExpress; cell line: BIN-67; collection date: not collected; common name: human; disease: ovarian small cell carcinoma; genotype: TP53-KO; geographic location (country and/or sea): not collected; isolate: not applicable; organism part: ovary; sample name: E-MTAB-15700:Sample 17; scientific_name: Homo sapiens
metadata_text: Sample 17 Homo sapiens BioSample: SAMEA120316716; SRA: ERS26978703 <BioSample access="public" publication_date="2026-05-06T00:00:00.000" last_update="2026-05-06T01:00:27.000" submission_date="2026-05-07T08:11:04.520" id="59255388" accession="SAMEA120316716"> <Ids> <Id db="BioSample" is_primary="1">SAMEA120316716</Id> <Id db="SRA">ERS26978703</Id> </Ids> <Description> <Title>Sample 17</Title> <Organism taxonomy_id="9606" taxonomy_name="Homo sapiens"> <OrganismName>Homo sapiens</OrganismName> </Organism> <Comment> <Paragraph>Protocols: BIN-67, BIN-67 p53ko, BIN-67 with SMARCA4 restoration and BIN-67 p53ko cells with SMARCA4 restoration were cultured in Roswell Park Memorial Institute 1640 Medium (RPMI, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 11875-093; no pyruvate) with 6% fetal bovine serum (Sigma, Cat# F1051), 1% penicillin/streptomycin (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 15140-122), and 2 mM L-glutamine (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 25030-081). Cells were maintained in a humidified 5% CO2-containing incubator at 37°C and regular Mycoplasma test was performed using Mycoalert Detection Kit (Lonza, Cat# LT07-318). Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Antibodies against H3K27ac (ab4729, Abcam), HDAC2 (ab124974, Abcam), p300 (ab14984, Abcam), HDAC1 (40967, Active Motif), p53(sc-126, Santa Cruz Biotechnology) were used for chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) experiments following a protocol with MNase. Briefly, the cell pellet was resuspended in 1 mL buffer A (25 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.5; 10 mmol/L KCl; 0.1% NP-40; and 1.5 mmol/L MgCl2) and homogenized. Nine volumes of buffer B (15 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.9; 15 mmol/L NaCl; 60 mmol/L KCl; 0.5 mmol/L phenylmethylsulfonylfluoride; and 0.32 mol/L sucrose) were added, and the mixture was centrifuged at 2,000 rpm (at 4°C for 7 minutes) to collect nuclei. The nuclei were resuspended in 1 mL buffer B and 3.3 µL of 1 mol/L CaCl2 and then prewarmed at 37°C for 15 minutes. MNase (1 µL, New England Biolabs, M0247S) was added and incubated at 37°C for 15 minutes. The reaction was quenched with 10 µmol/L EGTA on ice for 5 minutes. One volume of 2× buffer C (20 mmol/L Tris–HCl, pH 8.0; 200 mmol/L NaCl; 2 mmol/L EDTA; 1 mmol/L EGTA; 0.2% Na-deoxycholate; and 1% N-lauroylsarcosine) and Triton X-100 (final 1%) was added. Samples were then passed through a 21G needle five times and centrifuged at 13,000 g (4°C) for 15 minutes. Five micrograms of antibodies were added to the lysate for overnight incubation at 4°C. Protein G Magnetic Dynabeads (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were used for pulldown. Immunoprecipitated chromatin bound to Dynabeads was washed four times with RIPA buffer (50 mmol/L HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 500 mmol/L LiCl; 1 mmol/L EDTA; 1% NP-40; and 0.7% Na-deoxycholate). Chromatin was eluted from Dynabeads in 100 µL elution buffer (50 mmol/L Tris-HCl, pH 8.0; 10 mmol/L EDTA; and 1% SDS) by incubating at 65°C for 30 minutes. Immunoprecipitated samples and input were incubated overnight at 65°C to denature formaldehyde cross-linking. Samples were then treated with RNase A (Thermo Fisher Scientific), followed by proteinase K (Sigma-Aldrich).Genomic DNA was extracted using a Zymo kit(D5205, Zymo Research, Irvine, CA, USA). 1ug total DNA was used for the library construction. ChIP-sequencing (ChIP-seq) libraries were constructed using the NEBNext Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England Biolabs) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.</Paragraph> </Comment> </Description> <Owner> <Name>EBI</Name> </Owner> <Models> <Model>Generic</Model> </Models> <Package display_name="Generic">Generic.1.0</Package> <Attributes> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA first public">2026-05-06</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA-CHECKLIST">ERC000011</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC center name">McGill-D2R</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC status">public</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="Submitter Id">E-MTAB-15700:Sample 17</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="broker name">ArrayExpress</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="cell line" harmonized_name="cell_line" display_name="cell line">BIN-67</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="collection date" harmonized_name="collection_date" display_name="collection date">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="common name">human</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="disease" harmonized_name="disease" display_name="disease">ovarian small cell carcinoma</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="genotype" harmonized_name="genotype" display_name="genotype">TP53-KO</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="geographic location (country and/or sea)" harmonized_name="geo_loc_name" display_name="geographic location">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="isolate" harmonized_name="isolate" display_name="isolate">not applicable</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="organism part" harmonized_name="tissue" display_name="tissue">ovary</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="sample name" harmonized_name="sample_name" display_name="sample name">E-MTAB-15700:Sample 17</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="scientific_name">Homo sapiens</Attribute> </Attributes> <Status status="live" when="2026-05-08T08:19:48.160"/> </BioSample>

metadata_text: Sample 4 Homo sapiens BioSample: SAMEA120315783; SRA: ERS26977770 <BioSample access="public" publication_date="2026-05-06T00:00:00.000" last_update="2026-05-06T01:00:11.000" submission_date="2026-05-07T08:10:46.653" id="59254578" accession="SAMEA120315783"> <Ids> <Id db="BioSample" is_primary="1">SAMEA120315783</Id> <Id db="SRA">ERS26977770</Id> </Ids> <Description> <Title>Sample 4</Title> <Organism taxonomy_id="9606" taxonomy_name="Homo sapiens"> <OrganismName>Homo sapiens</OrganismName> </Organism> <Comment> <Paragraph>Protocols: BIN-67, BIN-67 with inducible SMARCA4 restoration were cultured in Roswell Park Memorial Institute 1640 Medium (RPMI, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 11875-093; no pyruvate) with 6% tetracycline free fetal bovine serum (Wisent Inc., Cat# 081150), 1% penicillin/streptomycin (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 15140-122), and 2 mM L-glutamine (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 25030-081). Cells were maintained in a humidified 5% CO2-containing incubator at 37°C and regular Mycoplasma test was performed using Mycoalert Detection Kit (Lonza, Cat# LT07-318). Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Antibodies against H3K27ac (ab4729, Abcam), HDAC2 (ab124974, Abcam), p300 (ab14984, Abcam), HDAC1 (40967, Active Motif), p53(sc-126, Santa Cruz Biotechnology) were used for chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) experiments following a protocol with MNase. Briefly, the cell pellet was resuspended in 1 mL buffer A (25 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.5; 10 mmol/L KCl; 0.1% NP-40; and 1.5 mmol/L MgCl2) and homogenized. Nine volumes of buffer B (15 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.9; 15 mmol/L NaCl; 60 mmol/L KCl; 0.5 mmol/L phenylmethylsulfonylfluoride; and 0.32 mol/L sucrose) were added, and the mixture was centrifuged at 2,000 rpm (at 4°C for 7 minutes) to collect nuclei. The nuclei were resuspended in 1 mL buffer B and 3.3 µL of 1 mol/L CaCl2 and then prewarmed at 37°C for 15 minutes. MNase (1 µL, New England Biolabs, M0247S) was added and incubated at 37°C for 15 minutes. The reaction was quenched with 10 µmol/L EGTA on ice for 5 minutes. One volume of 2× buffer C (20 mmol/L Tris–HCl, pH 8.0; 200 mmol/L NaCl; 2 mmol/L EDTA; 1 mmol/L EGTA; 0.2% Na-deoxycholate; and 1% N-lauroylsarcosine) and Triton X-100 (final 1%) was added. Samples were then passed through a 21G needle five times and centrifuged at 13,000 g (4°C) for 15 minutes. Five micrograms of antibodies were added to the lysate for overnight incubation at 4°C. Protein G Magnetic Dynabeads (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were used for pulldown. Immunoprecipitated chromatin bound to Dynabeads was washed four times with RIPA buffer (50 mmol/L HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 500 mmol/L LiCl; 1 mmol/L EDTA; 1% NP-40; and 0.7% Na-deoxycholate). Chromatin was eluted from Dynabeads in 100 µL elution buffer (50 mmol/L Tris-HCl, pH 8.0; 10 mmol/L EDTA; and 1% SDS) by incubating at 65°C for 30 minutes. Immunoprecipitated samples and input were incubated overnight at 65°C to denature formaldehyde cross-linking. Samples were then treated with RNase A (Thermo Fisher Scientific), followed by proteinase K (Sigma-Aldrich).Genomic DNA was extracted using a Zymo kit(D5205, Zymo Research, Irvine, CA, USA). 1ug total DNA was used for the library construction. ChIP-sequencing (ChIP-seq) libraries were constructed using the NEBNext Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England Biolabs) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.</Paragraph> </Comment> </Description> <Owner> <Name>EBI</Name> </Owner> <Models> <Model>Generic</Model> </Models> <Package display_name="Generic">Generic.1.0</Package> <Attributes> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA first public">2026-05-06</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA-CHECKLIST">ERC000011</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC center name">McGill-D2R</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC status">public</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="Submitter Id">E-MTAB-15699:Sample 4</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="broker name">ArrayExpress</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="cell line" harmonized_name="cell_line" display_name="cell line">BIN-67</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="collection date" harmonized_name="collection_date" display_name="collection date">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="common name">human</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="disease" harmonized_name="disease" display_name="disease">ovarian small cell carcinoma</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="genotype" harmonized_name="genotype" display_name="genotype">Control</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="geographic location (country and/or sea)" harmonized_name="geo_loc_name" display_name="geographic location">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="isolate" harmonized_name="isolate" display_name="isolate">not applicable</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="organism part" harmonized_name="tissue" display_name="tissue">ovary</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="sample name" harmonized_name="sample_name" display_name="sample name">E-MTAB-15699:Sample 4</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="scientific_name">Homo sapiens</Attribute> </Attributes> <Status status="live" when="2026-05-08T08:19:22.863"/> </BioSample>

attributes: ENA first public: 2026-05-06; ENA-CHECKLIST: ERC000011; INSDC center name: McGill-D2R; INSDC status: public; Submitter Id: E-MTAB-15700:Sample 16; broker name: ArrayExpress; cell line: BIN-67; collection date: not collected; common name: human; disease: ovarian small cell carcinoma; genotype: TP53-KO; geographic location (country and/or sea): not collected; isolate: not applicable; organism part: ovary; sample name: E-MTAB-15700:Sample 16; scientific_name: Homo sapiens
metadata_text: Sample 16 Homo sapiens BioSample: SAMEA120316715; SRA: ERS26978702 <BioSample access="public" publication_date="2026-05-06T00:00:00.000" last_update="2026-05-06T01:00:27.000" submission_date="2026-05-07T08:09:58.926" id="59252572" accession="SAMEA120316715"> <Ids> <Id db="BioSample" is_primary="1">SAMEA120316715</Id> <Id db="SRA">ERS26978702</Id> </Ids> <Description> <Title>Sample 16</Title> <Organism taxonomy_id="9606" taxonomy_name="Homo sapiens"> <OrganismName>Homo sapiens</OrganismName> </Organism> <Comment> <Paragraph>Protocols: BIN-67, BIN-67 p53ko, BIN-67 with SMARCA4 restoration and BIN-67 p53ko cells with SMARCA4 restoration were cultured in Roswell Park Memorial Institute 1640 Medium (RPMI, Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 11875-093; no pyruvate) with 6% fetal bovine serum (Sigma, Cat# F1051), 1% penicillin/streptomycin (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 15140-122), and 2 mM L-glutamine (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Cat# 25030-081). Cells were maintained in a humidified 5% CO2-containing incubator at 37°C and regular Mycoplasma test was performed using Mycoalert Detection Kit (Lonza, Cat# LT07-318). Cells were fixed in complete media with 0.3% formaldehyde for 30 minutes at 4°C and then quenched by adding 0.125 mol/L glycine for 5 minutes at room temperature and 15 minutes on ice. Fixed BIN-67 or BIN-67 TP53KO cells ± restoration of SMARCA4 were pelleted and washed once with 1× PBS before snap-freezing on dry ice. Antibodies against H3K27ac (ab4729, Abcam), HDAC2 (ab124974, Abcam), p300 (ab14984, Abcam), HDAC1 (40967, Active Motif), p53(sc-126, Santa Cruz Biotechnology) were used for chromatin immunoprecipitation (ChIP) experiments following a protocol with MNase. Briefly, the cell pellet was resuspended in 1 mL buffer A (25 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.5; 10 mmol/L KCl; 0.1% NP-40; and 1.5 mmol/L MgCl2) and homogenized. Nine volumes of buffer B (15 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.9; 15 mmol/L NaCl; 60 mmol/L KCl; 0.5 mmol/L phenylmethylsulfonylfluoride; and 0.32 mol/L sucrose) were added, and the mixture was centrifuged at 2,000 rpm (at 4°C for 7 minutes) to collect nuclei. The nuclei were resuspended in 1 mL buffer B and 3.3 µL of 1 mol/L CaCl2 and then prewarmed at 37°C for 15 minutes. MNase (1 µL, New England Biolabs, M0247S) was added and incubated at 37°C for 15 minutes. The reaction was quenched with 10 µmol/L EGTA on ice for 5 minutes. One volume of 2× buffer C (20 mmol/L Tris–HCl, pH 8.0; 200 mmol/L NaCl; 2 mmol/L EDTA; 1 mmol/L EGTA; 0.2% Na-deoxycholate; and 1% N-lauroylsarcosine) and Triton X-100 (final 1%) was added. Samples were then passed through a 21G needle five times and centrifuged at 13,000 g (4°C) for 15 minutes. Five micrograms of antibodies were added to the lysate for overnight incubation at 4°C. Protein G Magnetic Dynabeads (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were used for pulldown. Immunoprecipitated chromatin bound to Dynabeads was washed four times with RIPA buffer (50 mmol/L HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 500 mmol/L LiCl; 1 mmol/L EDTA; 1% NP-40; and 0.7% Na-deoxycholate). Chromatin was eluted from Dynabeads in 100 µL elution buffer (50 mmol/L Tris-HCl, pH 8.0; 10 mmol/L EDTA; and 1% SDS) by incubating at 65°C for 30 minutes. Immunoprecipitated samples and input were incubated overnight at 65°C to denature formaldehyde cross-linking. Samples were then treated with RNase A (Thermo Fisher Scientific), followed by proteinase K (Sigma-Aldrich).Genomic DNA was extracted using a Zymo kit(D5205, Zymo Research, Irvine, CA, USA). 1ug total DNA was used for the library construction. ChIP-sequencing (ChIP-seq) libraries were constructed using the NEBNext Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England Biolabs) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.</Paragraph> </Comment> </Description> <Owner> <Name>EBI</Name> </Owner> <Models> <Model>Generic</Model> </Models> <Package display_name="Generic">Generic.1.0</Package> <Attributes> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA first public">2026-05-06</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA-CHECKLIST">ERC000011</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC center name">McGill-D2R</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC status">public</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="Submitter Id">E-MTAB-15700:Sample 16</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="broker name">ArrayExpress</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="cell line" harmonized_name="cell_line" display_name="cell line">BIN-67</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="collection date" harmonized_name="collection_date" display_name="collection date">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="common name">human</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="disease" harmonized_name="disease" display_name="disease">ovarian small cell carcinoma</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="genotype" harmonized_name="genotype" display_name="genotype">TP53-KO</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="geographic location (country and/or sea)" harmonized_name="geo_loc_name" display_name="geographic location">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="isolate" harmonized_name="isolate" display_name="isolate">not applicable</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="organism part" harmonized_name="tissue" display_name="tissue">ovary</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="sample name" harmonized_name="sample_name" display_name="sample name">E-MTAB-15700:Sample 16</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="scientific_name">Homo sapiens</Attribute> </Attributes> <Status status="live" when="2026-05-08T08:18:22.627"/> </BioSample>

Nine volumes of buffer B (15 mmol/L HEPES–NaOH, pH 7.9; 15 mmol/L NaCl; 60 mmol/L KCl; 0.5 mmol/L phenylmethylsulfonylfluoride; and 0.32 mol/L sucrose) were added, and the mixture was centrifuged at 2,000 rpm (at 4°C for 7 minutes) to collect nuclei. The nuclei were resuspended in 1 mL buffer B and 3.3 µL of 1 mol/L CaCl2 and then prewarmed at 37°C for 15 minutes. MNase (1 µL, New England Biolabs, M0247S) was added and incubated at 37°C for 15 minutes. The reaction was quenched with 10 µmol/L EGTA on ice for 5 minutes. One volume of 2× buffer C (20 mmol/L Tris–HCl, pH 8.0; 200 mmol/L NaCl; 2 mmol/L EDTA; 1 mmol/L EGTA; 0.2% Na-deoxycholate; and 1% N-lauroylsarcosine) and Triton X-100 (final 1%) was added. Samples were then passed through a 21G needle five times and centrifuged at 13,000 g (4°C) for 15 minutes. Five micrograms of antibodies were added to the lysate for overnight incubation at 4°C. Protein G Magnetic Dynabeads (Thermo Fisher Scientific) were used for pulldown. Immunoprecipitated chromatin bound to Dynabeads was washed four times with RIPA buffer (50 mmol/L HEPES-KOH, pH 7.5; 500 mmol/L LiCl; 1 mmol/L EDTA; 1% NP-40; and 0.7% Na-deoxycholate). Chromatin was eluted from Dynabeads in 100 µL elution buffer (50 mmol/L Tris-HCl, pH 8.0; 10 mmol/L EDTA; and 1% SDS) by incubating at 65°C for 30 minutes. Immunoprecipitated samples and input were incubated overnight at 65°C to denature formaldehyde cross-linking. Samples were then treated with RNase A (Thermo Fisher Scientific), followed by proteinase K (Sigma-Aldrich).Genomic DNA was extracted using a Zymo kit(D5205, Zymo Research, Irvine, CA, USA). 1ug total DNA was used for the library construction. ChIP-sequencing (ChIP-seq) libraries were constructed using the NEBNext Ultra II DNA Library Prep Kit for Illumina (New England Biolabs) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.</Paragraph> </Comment> </Description> <Owner> <Name>EBI</Name> </Owner> <Models> <Model>Generic</Model> </Models> <Package display_name="Generic">Generic.1.0</Package> <Attributes> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA first public">2026-05-06</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="ENA-CHECKLIST">ERC000011</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC center name">McGill-D2R</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="INSDC status">public</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="Submitter Id">E-MTAB-15700:Sample 8</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="broker name">ArrayExpress</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="cell line" harmonized_name="cell_line" display_name="cell line">BIN-67</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="collection date" harmonized_name="collection_date" display_name="collection date">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="common name">human</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="disease" harmonized_name="disease" display_name="disease">ovarian small cell carcinoma</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="genotype" harmonized_name="genotype" display_name="genotype">SMARCA4</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="geographic location (country and/or sea)" harmonized_name="geo_loc_name" display_name="geographic location">not collected</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="isolate" harmonized_name="isolate" display_name="isolate">not applicable</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="organism part" harmonized_name="tissue" display_name="tissue">ovary</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="sample name" harmonized_name="sample_name" display_name="sample name">E-MTAB-15700:Sample 8</Attribute> <Attribute attribute_name="scientific_name">Homo sapiens</Attribute> </Attributes> <Status status="live" when="2026-05-08T08:18:10.673"/> </BioSample>

metadata_text: MIGS Eukaryotic sample from Cryptomonas Cryptomonas BioSample: SAMN56317056; Sample name: La Parguera Cryptomonad; SRA: SRS28315490 isolate: UKO-2021-05-05 <BioSample access="public" publication_date="2026-03-03T00:00:00.000" last_update="2026-03-07T01:06:22.700" submission_date="2026-03-03T23:37:04.830" id="56317056" accession="SAMN56317056"> <Ids> <Id db="BioSample" is_primary="1">SAMN56317056</Id> <Id db_label="Sample name">La Parguera Cryptomonad</Id> <Id db="SRA">SRS28315490</Id> </Ids> <Description> <Title>MIGS Eukaryotic sample from Cryptomonas</Title> <Organism taxonomy_id="3030" taxonomy_name="Cryptomonas"> <OrganismName>Cryptomonas</OrganismName> </Organism> <Comment> <Paragraph>The cryptophyte monoculture used for sequencing was founded from a single cell isolated on 5 May 2021 from a water sample collected in the La Parguera Bioluminescent Bay, Puerto Rico. The culture was maintained in a 2 L Corning PYREX #1395 round media storage bottle plugged with a cotton stopper to permit gas exchange while excluding external microorganisms. Growth medium consisted of 1.8 L distilled water, 64.8 g Instant Ocean sea salt, 234 uL Fritz Aquatics F/2 Algae Food Part A, 234 uL Part B, and 2160 uL of a 3.33 g/L sodium selenate stock solution. Transfers were performed weekly, and 0.306 g kanamycin was added during the first two weeks to ensure an axenic culture. Genomic DNA was extracted from actively growing cells and sequenced on the Illumina MiSeq platform using paired-end sequencing. Reads were quality-filtered with fastp and normalized with bbnorm prior to assembly.
